content: Planned action: keep the current per-tile split memory and size memory unchanged, then patch `app/widgets/page_matrix.py` so duo icons render larger and numbers disappear when a site or split is present in the top-bar buttons.

Planned action: verify the real current state of Topbrutus/MonDeuxiemeProjet after the user clarified the project is no longer the daily-issue-summary tool but the new project for parallel GPT-to-GPT talk. Inspect repo content before any repositioning or brain update.

Planned action: initialize Topbrutus/MonDeuxiemeProjet around its real goal: parallel GPT-to-GPT speak. Create a clean first scaffold with a project README, backlog, architecture note, gitignore, and starter Python modules for session, message bus, and orchestrator behavior.

Result: Topbrutus/MonDeuxiemeProjet now has a real scaffold aligned with the new goal of parallel GPT-to-GPT communication. Added a rewritten README, PROJECT-BACKLOG.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, .gitignore, and starter Python modules for session, message bus, orchestrator, and runnable demo entrypoint.

Planned action: make the current demo actually runnable and improve the transcript output. Fix the current `python src/main.py` run path so it does not depend on package-relative imports, then update orchestration and demo output for clear turn-based parallel speaking.

Result: the demo entrypoint is now runnable with `python src/main.py` and the transcript is cleaner. Updated src/main.py to support direct execution, updated src/orchestrator.py to show participants, waves, and synthesis, and added a run command to README.md.

Planned action: add a first real orchestration rule so some agents speak only after reading the previous wave. Update the orchestrator and demo to show a basic dependency flow instead of only free broadcast waves.

Result: MonDeuxiemeProjet now has a first real dependency rule. Updated src/orchestrator.py with `run_dependent_wave` so agents can speak from the previous turn context, updated src/main.py to demonstrate a seed wave plus a dependent review wave and a summary wave, and documented this new behavior in README.md.

Planned action: reconnect to Topbrutus/MonCerveauGPT, rebuild the Nino state from /brain/, verify the live ninoscreens repo and open work items, then resume from the frozen stable baseline where split works and durable restart persistence is the next target.

Planned action: patch the github-tracked ninoscreens baseline on a new branch so session_store is actually used at startup/close, tiles restore URL plus zoom across restart, and the current focus/split visibility state is restored. Explicitly note that the more advanced local split partner/ratio state described in the handoff is not yet present in the remote code baseline.

Result: the user resolved the conflicts cleanly in the GitHub UI. For `app/windows/main_window.py` the recommendation was to take incoming changes across the seven conflict blocks to preserve the more recent main structure.
Planned action: recall the current NINO RUN section state from the tracked srepo and resume from the last known context instead of starting from zero.

Planned action: reconnect to MonCerveauGPT third-eye first, then inspect Topbrutus/MonDeuxiemeProjet and the current Nino RUN workspace before implementing the end-to-end hub-centered approach with local command/result queues for `monprogramme`.
